title: Dryopteris uliginosa
intro: Dryopteris uliginosa, also known as the marsh fern or bog fern, is a species of fern native to temperate and boreal regions of the Northern Hemisphere. The health benefits of Dryopteris uliginosa include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Dryopteris uliginosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Dryopteris uliginosa is a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris, within the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to parts of Europe and North Africa. The classification of this plant is as follows: Kingdom - Plantae, Phylum - Pteridophyta, Class - Polypodiopsida, Order - Polypodiales, Family - Dryopteridaceae, Genus - Dryopteris, Species - D. uliginosa. The specific epithet 'uliginosa' refers to the plant's preference for wet environments. This fern is an important component of various ecosystems.
uses: Dryopteris uliginosa, also known as marsh wood fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. The plant's rhizomes and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects. Additionally, the plant is believed to have antiseptic and antifungal properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin infections. However, further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

constituents: Dryopteris uliginosa contains triterpenoids, including 23,24-dihydro-9α-hydroxy-3-oxo-urs-12-en-28-oic acid and 23,24-dihydro-3-oxo-urs-12-en-28-oic acid, as its active constituents. These compounds have been found to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's bioactive compounds also exhibit antimicrobial activity, which may contribute to its traditional use in folk medicine.
